title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement luke-jenkins-dev@outlook.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

what: Lightweight List Extensions that Enforce Unique Keys.
title: Lightweight List Extensions that Enforce Unique Keys.
text: Lightweight list extensions enforce unique keys.

title: Lightweight List Extensions that Enforce Unique Keys.
text: can install development version keylist like :

title: Lightweight List Extensions that Enforce Unique Keys.
text: keylist provides two lightweight keylist S3 classes klist knlist: extensions list enforce unique keys. klist accepts unnamed named elements whilst knlist accepts named elements. extensions list, keylists can take input list can. Inputs lists validated keylists - keylist validation operates level validation nested elements must also keylists. nested elements list can recursively turned keylist using one .* variants.
code: library(keylist)  # klist - unnamed or named (if names present, they must be unique). klist(1, a = 2) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> $a #> [1] 2 klist(a = 1, a = 2) #> Error in klist(a = 1, a = 2): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # knlist - fully named and unique. knlist(a = 1, b = 2) #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> $b #> [1] 2 knlist(1, b = 2) #> Error in knlist(1, b = 2): All elements must be named. knlist(a = 1, a = 2) #> Error in knlist(a = 1, a = 2): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # keylist as more general function. class(keylist(1)) #> [1] "klist" class(keylist(a = 1, .named = TRUE)) #> [1] "knlist" # input list separate entity. keylist(1, list(a = 1, a = 1)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 1  # each keylist validates its own level. keylist(1, keylist(a = 1, a = 1)) #> Error in klist(...): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a x <- list(1, list(a = 1, a = 1)) as.keylist(x, .recursive = TRUE) #> Error in as.klist.list(x, ..., .recursive = .recursive): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a

title: Lightweight List Extensions that Enforce Unique Keys.
text: Although lists R can accept duplicate names, R preferentially chooses existing list’s name structure comes assignment. Therefore can guarantee keys unique creation, way assigning duplicate key using normal R methods [, [[ $. See example: keylist makes use implementing special validation tricks - simply ensures keys unique upon creation let’s go way. However, assigning position enable non-named element knlist. circumvent (give clearer definition type), knlist objects allow assignment indexing done name. creation, main way duplicate names may creep lists direct name editing names() list concatenation c(). keylist includes S3 methods klist knlist functions ensure duplicate keys added methods.
code: x <- list(1, a = 2, 3, b = 4) x[1] <- list(x = 1) x[1] # remains unnamed. #> [[1]] #> [1] 1  x["a"] <- list(x = 1) x[2] # name remains 'a'. #> $a #> [1] 1  x[5] <- list(k = 10) # index 5 doesn't exist... x[5] # yet still remains unnamed. #> [[1]] #> [1] 10 x <- knlist(a = 1) x[[1]] <- 1 #> Error: Only character indexing is allowed for assignment into knlist objects  # extraction by index is allowed. x[[1]] #> [1] 1 x <- klist(1, a = 1) names(x) <- c("a", "a") #> Error in `names<-.klist`(`*tmp*`, value = c("a", "a")): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  x <- knlist(a = 1) # name removal allowed for klist, but not knlist. names(x) <- NULL #> Error in `names<-.knlist`(`*tmp*`, value = NULL): Names cannot be removed from a knlist object.  # knlist doesn't accept "" or NA names (like klist does). setNames(x, NA) #> Error in `names<-.knlist`(`*tmp*`, value = nm): Names cannot be NA.  class(c(klist(1, a = 2), 2, list(3))) #> [1] "klist" c(klist(1, a = 2), a = 2, list(3)) #> Error in c.klist(klist(1, a = 2), a = 2, list(3)): Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a

what: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
text: Create list unique keys (either fully named, knlist, mix named unnamed, klist), erroring duplicate keys found.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
code: keylist(..., .named = FALSE)  is.keylist(x)  as.keylist(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as.keylist(x, ..., .named = FALSE, .recursive = FALSE)  keylist.append(klst, values, after = length(klst))

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
text: ... keylist: objects, possibly named, .keylist: passed methods. .named boolean indicating whether list fully named . TRUE elements must named unique, FALSE names required present must unique. x object coerced tested. .recursive boolean indicating whether recursively validate nested lists. TRUE nested lists validated converted keylists, FALSE top level list validated converted keylist. klst passed append: keylist values appended. values passed append: values included modified keylist. passed append: subscript, values appended.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
text: list class klist knlist. .keylist() boolean.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
text: keylist() creates one two keylist objects: klist knlist, depending value .named argument. methods generally preferred explicit. .keylist() checks object inherits either klist knlist. keylist.append() applies append keylist validates result depending whether input klist knlist. keylist objects accept R object elements, keys must unique. key validation done top level, nested lists validated unless also keylists. recursively turn nested list structure keylists, use .keylist(x, .recursive = TRUE) one lower level variants.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
text: keylists compare names using C's strcmp function.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys — keylist
code: keylist(a = 1, 2, b = 3) # default is a klist #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> $b #> [1] 3 #>  try(keylist(1, a = 2, a = 1)) # duplicate keys not allowed #> Error in klist(...) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  x <- keylist(a = 1, b = 2, .named = TRUE) # create a knlist try(keylist(1, b = 2, .named = TRUE)) # unnamed keys not allowed #> Error in knlist(...) : All elements must be named. try(x[[1]] <- 1) # knlist only accepts character indexing for assignment #> Error : Only character indexing is allowed for assignment into knlist objects  # objects within a keylist are not subject to validation keylist(1, list(a = 1, a = 2)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 2 #>  #>  try(keylist(1, keylist(a = 1, a = 2))) # but nested keylists are #> Error in klist(...) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # recursively validate and convert to keylist x <- list(1, list(1, 2)) x <- as.keylist(x, .recursive = TRUE) class(x[[2]]) # nested list is now a keylist #> [1] "klist"  is.keylist(klist(1)) && is.keylist(knlist(a = 1)) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  keylist.append(klist(a = 1), list(2, b = 3)) # append to a klist #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> $b #> [1] 3 #>  # c() method for keylist objects also validates try(c(keylist(a = 1), list(a = 3))) #> Error in c.klist(keylist(a = 1), list(a = 3)) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a

code: klist(...)  is.klist(x)  as.klist(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as.klist(x, ..., .recursive = FALSE)  # S3 method for class 'klist' names(x) <- value  # S3 method for class 'klist' c(...)

title: Create a List With Unique Keys (Named or Unnamed) — klist
text: ... klist: objects, possibly named, .klist: passed methods. x object coerced tested. .recursive boolean indicating whether recursively validate nested lists. TRUE nested lists validated converted klists, FALSE top level list validated converted klist. value character vector length x, NULL. Resulting names must unique.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys (Named or Unnamed) — klist
text: list class klist. .klist() boolean.

title: Create a List With Unique Keys (Named or Unnamed) — klist

title: Create a List With Unique Keys (Named or Unnamed) — klist
code: klist(a = 1, 2, b = 3) #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 2 #>  #> $b #> [1] 3 #>  try(klist(1, a = 2, a = 1)) # duplicate keys not allowed #> Error in klist(1, a = 2, a = 1) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # objects within a klist are not subject to validation klist(1, list(a = 1, a = 2)) #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]]$a #> [1] 2 #>  #>  try(klist(1, klist(a = 1, a = 2))) # but nested klists are #> Error in klist(a = 1, a = 2) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # recursively validate and convert to klist x <- list(1, list(1, 2)) x <- as.klist(x, .recursive = TRUE) class(x[[2]]) # nested list is now a klist #> [1] "klist"  is.klist(klist(1)) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  try(names(x) <- c("a", "a")) # names are validated when changed #> Error in `names<-.klist`(`*tmp*`, value = c("a", "a")) :  #>   Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # c() method for klist objects also validates try(c(klist(a = 1), list(a = 3))) #> Error in c.klist(klist(a = 1), list(a = 3)) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a

title: Create a List With Unique Named Keys — knlist
code: x <- knlist(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3) try(knlist(b = 1, a = 2, a = 1)) # duplicate keys not allowed #> Error in knlist(b = 1, a = 2, a = 1) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a try(x[[1]] <- 1) # knlist only accepts character indexing for assignment #> Error : Only character indexing is allowed for assignment into knlist objects  # objects within a knlist are not subject to validation knlist(x = 1, list(a = 1, a = 2)) #> Error in knlist(x = 1, list(a = 1, a = 2)): All elements must be named. try(knlist(x = 1, knlist(a = 1, a = 2))) # but nested knlists are #> Error in knlist(a = 1, a = 2) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # recursively validate and convert to knlist x <- list(a = 1, b = list(x = 1, y = 2)) x <- as.knlist(x, .recursive = TRUE) class(x[[2]]) # nested list is now a knlist #> [1] "knlist"  is.knlist(knlist(a = 1)) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  try(names(x) <- c("a", "a")) # names are validated when changed #> Error in `names<-.knlist`(`*tmp*`, value = c("a", "a")) :  #>   Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a  # c() method for knlist objects also validates try(c(knlist(a = 1), list(a = 3))) #> Error in c.knlist(knlist(a = 1), list(a = 3)) : Names must be unique. #> Duplicate name: a
